page: Microsoft Azure E16bds_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the EBDSv5 family. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, utilizing Microsoft Hyper-V as the hypervisor. This instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It includes 614 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, optimized for high block storage performance. No GPUs are present on this server.

Benchmark evaluations place the E16bds_v5 in the average performance tier across multiple workloads. In CPU-focused tests, it achieves a stress-ng single-core score of 1899.09 and a multi-core score of 15534.8, supported by a PassMark CPU score of 21141.48. The memory subsystem demonstrates a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 839.97 GB/sec, which drops to 102.72 GB/sec for large block uncached reads. Cryptographic and compression capabilities are solid, with a PassMark encryption score of 14607.72 and single-threaded gzip compression throughput of 28.53 GB/sec.

Given its high memory density and local storage capacity, the E16bds_v5 offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that do not require GPU acceleration. It is configured for database operations, as reflected in its PassMark database score of 7921.71 and a Redis SET throughput of 15.88 million operations per second. Additionally, it handles moderate web serving demands, achieving a static web serving throughput of 20.29 GB/sec, and small-scale language model inference, processing 1009.79 tokens per second for a 135M parameter model.
description: Microsoft Azure E16bds_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the EBDSv5 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs (8 physical cores) running at 2.8 GHz, 128.0 GB of RAM, and 614 GB of local storage. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance is designed for high block storage performance and is Premium Storage capable. Benchmark data indicates solid mid-range performance, with a PassMark CPU score of 21141.48 and a Redis SET throughput of 15.88 million operations per second. The server's high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core makes it qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy applications. It is configured for relational databases, in-memory caching, data processing, and moderate web serving workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16bds_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and 614 GB of local storage, designed for database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16bds_v5 is a memory-optimized VM with 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 614 GB local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and local storage for demanding database and caching workloads.

128.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU memory ratio
